documentation_accuracy_summary: AGENTS.md's new 'Continuous Integration' subsection (lines 168-170) accurately describes the workflow: push/PR to master plus workflow_dispatch triggers, ubuntu-24.04 runner, the same make/make check commands documented above, and the wx 3.3.2 source-build + actions/cache rationale (Ubuntu 24.04 apt only ships wx 3.2.4) -- all independently verified against the actual .github/workflows/ci.yml contents.

doc/synthesized-roadmap.md Part III item #3 (line 700) is updated from 'RESOLVED (build-system portion)' to plain 'RESOLVED', with an annotation crediting P3-1/P3-2/P3-3/P3-4 and explicitly describing the CI deliverable (ubuntu-24.04, wx 3.3.2 source build, actions/cache). Verified against the roadmap's own item #3 text (scoped to 'make check target + minimal CI; pin -std=c++17 both platforms; common.mk') that all four stated sub-deliverables are now complete, and that the deliberately-deferred items (Windows ConformanceMode flip, FBattleScreen/FBattleBoard/FBattleDisplay->src/gui relocation, CMake adoption) were never part of item #3's original scope, so upgrading the qualifier to unqualified RESOLVED is accurate rather than overclaiming.
No duplication or contradiction found between AGENTS.md and doc/synthesized-roadmap.md's descriptions of the CI workflow; CLAUDE.md correctly contains no build/CI facts (SSOT rule preserved).
test_sufficiency_summary: This is a declarative GitHub Actions YAML deliverable with no C++ runtime surface; the plan's own Validation/Tester-handoff sections anticipated that GitHub Actions cannot be executed in this sandboxed environment, so no CppUnit fixtures apply and none were added (Tester commit: 'No Changes Made').

The Tester's verification goes beyond bare source-string matching: it parses the workflow through a real YAML parser into a structured document and asserts on the resulting parsed structure (trigger branches, job count/runner, step run-block contents, permissions dict equality, SHA-pin regex match against parsed `uses:` values) -- this is execution of a real parser against the artifact and assertion on its actual resolved output, not a textual grep.

Independently re-executed this verification during this review: `yaml.safe_load` on .github/workflows/ci.yml resolves push.branches=[master], pull_request.branches=[master], workflow_dispatch present, workflow-level and job-level permissions both {contents: read}, a single ubuntu-24.04 job with 8 steps including bare 'make' and 'make check' run steps, and confirmed the literal substring 'secrets.' is absent from the raw file.

Independently confirmed both pinned action SHAs are live and correct via `git ls-remote --tags`: actions/checkout 11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 == refs/tags/v4.2.2; actions/cache 1bd1e32a3bdc45362d1e726936510720a7c30a57 == refs/tags/v4.2.0 -- both match their trailing version-comment claims exactly, not just 'reachable'.

Independently confirmed AC4 by reading the repo-root Makefile directly (not just trusting the Tester's claim): `default: all`, `all:` builds src+tests, `check: all` runs SSWTests, tactical/TacticalTests, and gui/GuiTests under xvfb-run -a. The workflow's bare `make` and `make check` steps invoke exactly these targets with no CI-only divergence.
actionlint unavailability was independently reconfirmed in this environment (`which actionlint` -> exit 1), consistent with the Implementer's and Tester's stated limitation.

Given the inherent inability to execute GitHub Actions in this sandbox, this combination of real-parser structural assertions, live external reachability/tag verification, and direct Makefile comparison is the strongest available verification and is treated as adequate -- not a source-inspection-only violation of the mandatory behavioral-test policy, which targets C++ application runtime behavior and does not have an executable local equivalent for a cloud-CI YAML artifact.
No coverage gaps identified relative to the five acceptance criteria.
